note: Enforcement coverage register: every contract obligation names the mechanical surface that enforces it, or carries a declared exception with a recorded basis. Surface kinds: commit-gate (hooks/pre-commit + harness precommit), ci (workflow step), gate (validate-*.mjs in validate-all), contract-test (tests/contracts), runtime-adapter (per-family runtime hooks/statusline; capability recorded in agents.json runtimeEnforcement), review-lens (independent critic checks it; basis names the lens), prose-declared (no mechanical surface exists; basis records why and what bounds the risk). validate-enforcement-map.mjs enforces the shape, path existence, and the required-obligation floor. Adding an obligation to the contract without adding it here is a validation failure by review convention; a prose-declared surface without a basis is a validation failure mechanically.

obligations.obligation: Tier is computed from touched paths, never self-declared.
Non-trivial work has an execution ledger whose Affected Paths cover the changed paths; an unrelated ledger does not satisfy the requirement.

High-risk commits are commit claims and require critic terminal evidence (scoped pushback-free report or terminal dual-role ledger status); mechanical version adoption in targets is priced via the upgrade-adoption commit shape.
Every response opens with the [harness] tier line.

surfaces.basis: Codex CLI exposes no statusline or prompt/response hook surface as of 2026-07-03 (agents.json runtimeEnforcement.basis); for that family the obligation is contract prose reinforced at review time. Compliance telemetry (visibility-compliance.jsonl) measures drift where the responseCheck adapter runs.

obligations.obligation: Governed work adds one line per harness-meaningful transition (tool run, skill loaded, normative doc consulted, gate result).

surfaces.basis: Which tool runs are harness-meaningful is a semantic judgment no transcript regex can decide without flooding false blocks, so no response-check surface exists for narration as of 2026-07-03. Bounded by: the per-turn UserPromptSubmit re-injection names the narration duty alongside the tier line; the statusline carries the governed state (version, tier, ledger, critic) continuously and independently of narration; and transcript spot-checks are a review-lens item on high-risk work. Distinct from visibility-tier-line, whose opening-line half IS mechanically enforced.

obligations.obligation: The repo being governed by a bootstrapped harness (version, tier, ledger, critic state) is visible independently of model output.
Canonical validation runs before completion claims; completed ledgers record the evidence.
Readiness requires critic terminal evidence with no unresolved pushback; nonBlockingRisks are resolved or accepted-with-rationale in the ledger.
Non-trivial work records a primary performer and an independent critic; cross-family for high-risk when both families are available; critic models never downgraded.
Review transport policy is per-family registry data with measured ceilings; degradation is priced; parsing is echo-hardened.
Constants that gate behavior live in registry/profile data with a recorded basis, never bare literals in scripts.

surfaces.basis: Required lens item for framework-change reviews (review.md); a magic number in a gate or transport path is a blocking finding. Mechanical detection of 'which literals are operational' is not tractable; the registry integrity gates enforce basis fields where the data already lives.

obligations.obligation: Destructive operations (source deletion, git reset, force push, harness replacement, secret scope violations) checkpoint with the user first.

surfaces.basis: Real-time interception belongs to each runtime's permission layer (framework/process/permission-profiles.md); repo content cannot pre-empt a shell command and must never claim to bypass or replace permission enforcement. Bounded by: permission profiles at the runtime, secret-scan gate over tracked artifacts (validate-secrets.mjs), and cleanup guardrails checked at review time.

obligations.obligation: Gates are never skippable; HARNESS_SKIP is a loud local escape only — CI still enforces the same tier gate on the pushed diff.
Generated skill adapters (.claude/skills, .codex/skills, skills-ref) exactly match framework/skills-src; stale, hand-edited, or orphaned outputs fail.
Framework/ is portable and override/ is target-owned; the distributable surface is manifest-declared; development/ never ships.
History is never rewritten; pre-upgrade completed plans are exempt only via the git-anchored acceptance file.
Feature work in a target is blocked until the intake artifact is ready.
Targets record the governing harness version in the target-owned adoption record; the statusline reads it and the commit gate anchors the upgrade-adoption shape to it.
Critique the ask; converge before delivering; verified verdicts on challenges — user challenges enter the review ledger as critique findings.

surfaces.basis: Judgment behaviors are not mechanically checkable from repo state. Bounded by: ledger gates require dual-role critic fields to be meaningfully filled (validate-exec-plans.mjs) and high-risk plans to carry the Critique And Debate annex (validate-cross-agent-review.mjs); convergence evidence is recorded via the artifact-contract annex when deliverable-tier, the independent critic reviews intent alignment as a required lens, and per-turn runtime re-injection keeps the obligations in the working set for capable families.

obligations.obligation: Durable facts live in tracked artifacts; runtime memory may cache or point, never own; resume state precedes the step that needs it.

surfaces.basis: Runtime memories are outside repo reach; placement is checked at review time (review.md lenses) and structurally encouraged by the Current Step pointer and ledger-before-edits commit gate. Entropy checks sweep stale artifacts (framework/process/gates.md).

obligations.obligation: A conformant core repo has the canonical layout (agent/<pkg>, channels/<declared>, tools/, modal_app.py, docs/, tests/, harness/); transport and agent-package divergence is declared, never discovered.

surfaces.basis: Placement rules (transports only in channels/, brain only in agent/, domain work in tools/) concern code content the path gate cannot verify; reviewers and critics check them, and repoType/conformance honesty is checked at harness-upgrade review time as the contract records.

obligations.obligation: A conformant core repo shares one toolchain surface: pyproject.toml as dependency + tooling source of truth, and a Makefile defining test, lint, validate, dry-run.

surfaces.basis: File existence and Makefile verb names are gated, but recipe honesty (the lint target actually invokes the declared linter — the motivating flake8-after-ruff drift — not just a target named lint), dependency-source honesty (requirements.txt derived from pyproject, deps not buried only in modal_app.py), and the pytest/Node suite split are content judgments checked by reviewers, since the gate reads target names, not what the recipe runs.

obligations.obligation: Generated output (validation reports, run logs, caches, scratch state) stays in gitignored directories and is not committed structure.

surfaces.basis: The gate exempts gitignored directories from divergence and refuses to launder force-added tracked files inside them, but whether a given reports/ file is a committed fixture or accumulated run residue is a content judgment only a reviewer can make; keep-it-gitignored is checked at review time and by entropy sweeps.

obligations.obligation: Agent-facing instruction surfaces (skills, process docs, templates, entrypoint prose) follow the authoring standard: per-sentence no-op test, Done when: criteria on ordered steps, description formula, disclosure/placement rules, and the narrative-vs-evidence durability split.

surfaces.basis: High-risk and framework-affecting reviews apply the coherence lens plus a writing-skills no-op/done-when audit to every changed agent-facing prose surface; the drift gate pins skills-src as the only editable skill surface so the audited source is authoritative.

Sentence-level no-op judgments are model-relative and settled empirically by running the skill; they are not decidable from repo state, so the standard itself remains prose with critic review as the enforcement path.

obligations.obligation: Non-trivial work decomposes into vertical slices: each implementation step cuts through every integration layer end-to-end, dependency-ordered with the riskiest integration first, HITL vs agent-autonomous classified.

surfaces.basis: Plan reviews and the independent critic check implementation-step shape against the slice rules; slice quality is a judgment over the plan's step list and the codebase's layer boundaries, checked at planning and review time.

Whether a step is a genuine tracer bullet is not decidable from repo state before the work exists; the ledger's Implementation Steps section records the decomposition for reviewers, and gates.md carries the GOOD/BAD contrast pair.
